beats.activity: Wake up, shower, coffee, and light breakfast at home.
beats.reasoning: Wei wakes with full energy on a sunny Monday. Morning routine is quick and efficient—she's a planner and will map out her day while having coffee.

beats.activity: Drive from home to first hospice patient visit in Bellevue.
beats.reasoning: Wei's workplace is Bellevue downtown and she does home visits. Light Rail is closed 9am–3pm, so she drives early to avoid the closure window. Early arrival also gives her buffer time.

beats.activity: First hospice patient home visit: assess vitals, medication review, emotional support.
beats.reasoning: Wei begins her day with a patient visit. As a hospice nurse, her work is deeply meaningful and requires presence. She schedules first visit early to avoid midday heat (74F high today).

beats.activity: Drive to second patient location in nearby Bellevue neighborhood.
beats.reasoning: Wei travels between patient homes within the Bellevue area. She keeps visits clustered geographically to maximize efficiency and minimize driving time.

beats.activity: Second hospice patient home visit: wound care, pain management, family conversation.
beats.reasoning: Wei's second patient visit of the morning. She's intentional about spending adequate time with each patient. The work is demanding but aligns with her values.

beats.activity: Lunch break: grab a salad at a local café, check spreadsheet of weekly expenses.
beats.reasoning: Wei takes a structured lunch break at midday. She's meticulous about tracking expenses and uses downtime to update her spreadsheet. Whole Foods is convenient and has healthy options.

beats.activity: Drive to third patient home in Bellevue.
beats.reasoning: Another short drive to her final afternoon patient. Wei clusters her visits to minimize commute time and preserve energy for patient care.

beats.activity: Third hospice patient home visit: symptom management and family support.
beats.reasoning: Wei's third and final patient visit of the day. She allocates 90 minutes to ensure quality time and thorough care. This completes her morning/early afternoon work block.

beats.activity: Drive home from Bellevue; light traffic on 405 southbound.
beats.reasoning: Wei heads home mid-afternoon. The Light Rail closure (9am–3pm) is now lifted, but she's already in her car. Early departure from Bellevue avoids rush-hour congestion.

beats.activity: Rest and hydrate at home; review notes from patient visits.
beats.reasoning: Wei arrives home with time to decompress. Hospice work is emotionally taxing, and she uses this period to process and hydrate before evening. The sunny 74F day is pleasant; she's not interested in the FIFA match.

beats.activity: Evening exercise: salsa dancing class at a local studio.
beats.reasoning: Wei is passionate about salsa dancing and uses it as a outlet to process the day's emotional weight. The sunny weather and evening timing make it an ideal wind-down activity. She drives north to Fremont for her regular Monday class.

beats.activity: Drive back home from dance class.
beats.reasoning: Wei returns home from Fremont with enough time to prepare a light dinner and wind down for the evening.

diary:
Monday was one of those days where the weight of the work settles into your bones. Started before dawn like always—shower, coffee, the quiet ritual that keeps me grounded. Three home visits in Bellevue, all back-to-back. The first patient's daughter was there, asking questions I've answered a hundred times but somehow still need to hear. The second family wanted to talk about logistics, about what comes next. I listened more than I spoke.

Grabbed a salad at Whole Foods around lunch, tried to check my budget spreadsheet but honestly just stared at it for a minute. The numbers felt abstract compared to the real weight of the morning.

By evening I needed to move. Drove out to Fremont for salsa class—something about the music and the rhythm pulled me out of myself. For ninety minutes I was just a body in motion, not carrying anyone else's grief. Drove home under the long June twilight feeling a little more like myself.
thought_process: I chose to focus the diary on the emotional texture rather than listing every visit—that's the real story for me on days like this. The World Cup and transit closures barely registered because I was already locked into my Bellevue route and honestly, work overshadowed everything else. My mood shifted noticeably around lunchtime when I hit a wall, that moment of numbness. But I intentionally prioritized the dance class. It wasn't just scheduling; it was a conscious choice to protect my own wellbeing. I've learned that if I don't move and feel joy in my body, the job consumes me entirely. The salsa became necessary, not optional.
world_event_prompt: Sunny Monday in early June, high of 74F. FIFA World Cup group stage match (USA vs Wales) airs at 11am PDT — bars downtown open early. Light Rail closed between Westlake and University District 9am-3pm for maintenance.
